author: gb9t
content:
Identified by 00-rust-conventions.md concurrency-primitives table — abstractions Rookery needs that aren't in upstream rust-for-linux at baseline 27a26ccfd528:

1. RwSemaphore<T> — sleepable reader-writer lock; wraps C 'rw_semaphore'. Tier 3 design doc + TLA+ model (proves no-starvation + writer-priority invariants).
2. Kthread (kernel::task::Kthread or similar) — safe Rust API for spawning kernel threads. Today rust/kernel/task.rs only wraps the existing task_struct handle. Tier 3 design doc + Kani harness for stop/wakeup safety.
3. PerCpu<T> — safe per-CPU container. rust/kernel/cpu.rs has CPU-id helpers but no container. Tier 3 design doc + harness + TLA+ model (because per-CPU access interacts with preemption).

Each becomes a subissue with its own subsystem doc when Phase B reaches the relevant subsystem (kernel/locking, kernel/, kernel/sched).

Block by issue #1.
